evals.prompt: Refactor the helloworld sample using jq++. The sample is at samples/helloworld/. Place the refactored sources under samples/.refactored/helloworld/, shared base files under samples/.refactored/shared/, and create a generate.sh. Run generate.sh to produce samples/.generated/helloworld/, verify the output matches the original, and report metrics (lines and words before/after) plus any interesting findings.
evals.expected_output: Refactored .yaml++ source files with $extends inheritance for the two near-identical Deployments, a generate.sh that correctly reproduces helloworld.yaml, verification PASS, and a metrics report.

assertions.name: semantic_equivalence
assertions.description: yq -o=json of original and generated helloworld.yaml produce identical JSON (ignoring formatting)

assertions.name: uses_extends_or_local
assertions.description: At least one .yaml++ file under samples/.refactored/helloworld/ contains '$extends' or '$local'

assertions.name: line_reduction
assertions.description: Total lines in .yaml++ sources <= total lines in original .yaml files

assertions.name: two_deployments_share_base
assertions.description: Both deployment .yaml++ files reference a common base file via $extends (the key v1/v2 refactoring)

evals.prompt: Refactor the sleep sample using jq++. The sample is at samples/sleep/. Place the refactored sources under samples/.refactored/sleep/, shared base files under samples/.refactored/shared/, and create a generate.sh. Run generate.sh to produce samples/.generated/sleep/, verify the output matches the original, and report metrics (lines and words before/after) plus any interesting findings.
evals.expected_output: Refactored .yaml++ source files, a generate.sh that correctly reproduces sleep.yaml, verification PASS, and a metrics report noting the app-name parametrization pattern.

assertions.name: semantic_equivalence
assertions.description: yq -o=json of original and generated sleep.yaml produce identical JSON (ignoring formatting)

assertions.name: uses_extends_or_local
assertions.description: At least one .yaml++ file under samples/.refactored/sleep/ contains '$extends' or '$local'

assertions.name: line_reduction
assertions.description: Total lines in .yaml++ sources <= total lines in original .yaml files

assertions.name: shared_base_referenced
assertions.description: At least one file in samples/.refactored/shared/ is referenced from sleep's .yaml++ sources

evals.prompt: Refactor the curl sample using jq++. The sample is at samples/curl/. Place the refactored sources under samples/.refactored/curl/, shared base files under samples/.refactored/shared/, and create a generate.sh. Run generate.sh to produce samples/.generated/curl/, verify the output matches the original, and report metrics (lines and words before/after) plus any interesting findings.
evals.expected_output: Refactored .yaml++ source files reusing shared bases (ideally overlapping with sleep's shared base), a generate.sh that correctly reproduces curl.yaml, verification PASS, and a metrics report.

assertions.name: semantic_equivalence
assertions.description: yq -o=json of original and generated curl.yaml produce identical JSON (ignoring formatting)

assertions.name: uses_extends_or_local
assertions.description: At least one .yaml++ file under samples/.refactored/curl/ contains '$extends' or '$local'

assertions.name: line_reduction
assertions.description: Total lines in .yaml++ sources <= total lines in original .yaml files

assertions.name: shared_base_referenced
assertions.description: At least one file in samples/.refactored/shared/ is referenced from curl's .yaml++ sources
